observable_failures: Requests to /.well-known/webfinger return HTTP 404 — no such endpoint exists
The /.well-known/change-password route is embedded in a user-specific route file, not a .well-known-specific router, reducing maintainability
repair_targets: Implement GET /.well-known/webfinger endpoint returning WebFinger-compliant JSON (subject, aliases, links)
Create src/controllers/well-known.js with webfinger handler including resource validation, authorization check, user resolution, and JSON response construction
Create src/routes/well-known.js defining both /.well-known/webfinger and /.well-known/change-password routes
Move /.well-known/change-password redirect from user-specific route module to src/routes/well-known.js
Integrate new well-known router into main route composition at app startup
regression_expectations: Existing route definitions must continue to work after changes
The /.well-known/change-password redirect must still redirect to /me/edit/password after being moved
Authentication and authorization system for other endpoints must remain unaffected
The privileges API must remain functional for other endpoints using groups:view:users permission

semantic_boundaries: webfinger function accepts req (Express Request) with query.param.resource and res (Express Response)
Route module signature: (app, middleware, controllers) → void
Authorization gated by groups:view:users global permission via existing privileges API
Resource param format: acct:username@hostname where hostname == nconf.get('url_parsed').hostname
behavioral_constraints: TO-BE: webfinger handler must return HTTP 400 if resource query param is missing
TO-BE: webfinger handler must return HTTP 400 if resource does not begin with acct:
TO-BE: webfinger handler must return HTTP 400 if resource does not end with nconf.get('url_parsed').hostname
TO-BE: webfinger handler must return HTTP 404 if the user identified by the username portion of resource cannot be resolved
TO-BE: webfinger handler must return HTTP 403 if req.uid lacks the groups:view:users global permission
TO-BE: for unauthenticated requests (req.uid absent), authorization must be evaluated as the Guest role using the existing privileges API
TO-BE: on valid requests, response JSON must include subject field set to the original resource string
TO-BE: on valid requests, response JSON aliases array must contain both UID-based and slug-based user profile URLs
TO-BE: on valid requests, response JSON links array must include at least one entry referencing the user's HTML profile page (canonical /user/<slug> URL)
TO-BE: /.well-known/change-password must redirect requests to /me/edit/password independently of user authentication context
backward_compatibility: The /.well-known/change-password redirect behavior must be preserved after being moved from the user-specific route module to src/routes/well-known.js
similar_implementation_patterns: Use existing privileges API (similar to other permission-gated endpoints) for Guest role authorization check
Follow existing NodeBB route/controller file pattern (src/controllers/<module>.js + src/routes/<module>.js)
Integrate into main route composition at app startup, similar to other route modules
missing_elements_to_implement: src/controllers/well-known.js:webfinger(req: Request, res: Response) — handles GET /.well-known/webfinger, validates resource, checks authorization via privileges API (Guest role for unauthenticated), resolves username/slug to UID, returns { subject, aliases, links }

src/routes/well-known.js: route configuration module — mounts /.well-known/change-password (redirect to /me/edit/password) and GET /.well-known/webfinger delegating to controllers['well-known'].webfinger

suspect_entities: src/routes/well-known.js (new file — does not exist yet)
src/controllers/well-known.js (new file — does not exist yet)
User-specific route module where /.well-known/change-password is currently embedded (requires removal of redirect logic)
Main route composition file (requires integration of new well-known route)
src/routes/well-known.js (new file — already exists untracked, already wired in routes/index.js)
src/controllers/well-known.js (new file — already exists untracked, already implemented)
src/controllers/index.js (well-known controller NOT registered — inconsistency with other controllers)
src/controllers/well-known.js (webfinger function, lines 12-49; changePassword function, lines 8-10)

call_chain_context: src/start.js -> src/webserver.js -> src/routes/index.js -> _mounts.wellKnown(app, middleware, controllers) -> src/routes/well-known.js -> controllers['well-known'].webfinger
dataflow_relevant_uses: src/controllers/well-known.js:24-25: const url = nconf.get('url'); new URL(url).hostname for hostname extraction
src/controllers/well-known.js:31: const uid = await user.getUidByUsername(username) for user lookup
src/controllers/well-known.js:36: const userslug = await user.getUserField(uid, 'userslug') for profile URL construction

src/privileges/global.js:58: view:users -> groups:view:users automatic group key mapping

must_co_edit_relations: New src/routes/well-known.js must be integrated into main route composition (app startup)
The /.well-known/change-password redirect logic must be REMOVED from the user-specific route module when added to src/routes/well-known.js
If src/routes/well-known.js is modified — no other files need co-edits, already wired
If src/controllers/index.js changes — well-known controller not registered there (inconsistency to fix)
Controllers.wellKnown registration in src/controllers/index.js requires ONLY adding one line: Controllers.wellKnown = require('./well-known') — no other file changes needed
src/routes/well-known.js and src/controllers/well-known.js must be committed together (already paired)
src/routes/user.js does NOT contain /.well-known/change-password — no removal needed from user routes

Hostname validation for resource param depends on nconf.get('url_parsed').hostname
New well-known router must be integrated into main route composition and mounted during app startup

user module -> src/controllers/well-known.js lines 4, 31, 36 for user resolution and slug lookup
